what_it_does: Defines an ITEM_TYPE dictionary for the note item type, specifying its id, label, long description, initial state, allowed states, and state transitions.
observations: The note is described as lighter than a tip and distinct from a comment, serving as an observation or annotation attached to any addressed content.
It is threadable: replies can link reply_to a note.
Lifecycle has two states, posted and archived, with initial state posted.
Transitions are fully reversible: posted can go to archived and archived can return to posted.
standouts: Explicit semantic differentiation from tip and comment.
Threadable via reply_to, suggesting a conversation structure.
conventions: Uses an ITEM_TYPE dictionary constant with id, label, desc, initial, states, and transitions keys.
Long description split across concatenated string literals.
State machine expressed as adjacency lists keyed by state.
concerns: The archived -> posted transition is unguarded in this file; semantics of who can restore or when are not defined here.
Comparisons to tip and comment reference types not present in this file, so local meaning is incomplete.
No validation rules, permissions, or reply_to field schema are included.
notes: This file is a small schema declaration for a lightweight, threaded, archivable note item type.
questions: How is reply_to represented in the data model for this item type?
What distinguishes a tip from a note or a comment elsewhere in the system?
Are there permissions or conditions governing the archived -> posted transition?
Does archived imply soft deletion, hiding, or something else?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file content: it declares a note item type with a simple lifecycle and semantic description.
intention_signals: It appears designed to register a minimal, reusable annotation type that can attach to any addressed content, support threaded replies, and be archived and restored rather than deleted.
novelty: The explicit distinction from tip and comment, plus a reversible two-state lifecycle, is distinctive for such a simple schema file.
contribution: It contributes a self-contained item-type schema for notes, including semantic distinctions from tips and comments and a simple archive/unarchive lifecycle, which a fused system can keep or align with its annotation models.
summary_for_embedding: This file declares the note item type schema with id, label, description, initial state posted, states posted and archived, and reversible transitions.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to define a lightweight, threadable, archivable annotation item type for addressed content.
